other_observers: Scott Shaum refound the bird and it was seen by   Joyce T. Brandon Percival Charles Thronton-Kolbe Glenn Walbek John Drummond two Shaum boys Dawn Moon and later by Mark Peterson and David Chartier; I am the original.
duration: first time about one minute, second time on and off for about three minutes
specific_location: The dam of Sherrie's, Little Headquarters Pond, Chico Basin Ranch. later above the nearby ranch house.
description: The bird was yellow underneath with verticle black marks from an necklace on its breast. The back was blue-gray. The face was stricking especially the bold eyering which had yellow on the top part and yellowish white on the bottom of the eyering. Just behind the bill was a black patch which extended up to the front of the crown.  The lore was yellow contrasting with a black patch just below the eye, which extended to the yellow chin and throat, making it a very stricking bird. The black "necklace" broke up the yellow of the throat and breast.Behaviors: First found foraging low, only a couple feet off the ground and then a minute later foraging in small willow about five feet off the ground. Very nervous, fidgety feeder.   Later I saw it move about high in a Siberian elm.Call: non heardPlumage: adult
